items.title: Migrate codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
items.body:
Both `main.py` and `experimental.py` used Python 2-only constructs (`izip`, `print` statements, `raw_input`, unguarded `map()`). This migrates the codebase to Python 3.

### `main.py`
- Drop `izip` import; use built-in `zip`
- `print "..."` → `print(...)`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`
- `map(int, ...)` → `list(map(int, ...))` (map is lazy in Py3)
- Integer division: `/` → `//`

### `experimental.py`
- `raw_input(...)` → `input(...)`

### `README.md`
- Requirement updated from Python 2 → Python 3
- Commands updated from `python` → `python3`

items.title: Migrate to async SQLAlchemy 2.0
items.body:
## Summary

- Migrated entire DB layer from synchronous SQLAlchemy (`create_engine` + `Session`) to async SQLAlchemy 2.0 (`create_async_engine` + `AsyncSession`)
- All ~60 route handlers, ~30 service functions, and 6 background pollers now use non-blocking async DB I/O
- Eliminates event loop starvation that caused 15-20 second intermittent page load delays (7 pollers were executing 10+ blocking DB calls/sec on a single-threaded uvicorn worker)

## Changes (31 files, +637/-608)

**Phase 0**: Converted 5 legacy `db.query().delete()` calls to SA 2.0 `delete()` statements; added `aiosqlite` dep

**Phase 1**: New async engine + session factory in `app/db.py` with `expire_on_commit=False`

**Phase 2**: Converted all route handlers and service functions — mechanical `db.scalars()` → `await db.scalars()` transformation across 15 files

**Phase 3**: Replaced `asyncio.to_thread(SessionLocal())` pattern in all 6 pollers with native `async with AsyncSessionLocal()`

**Phase 4**: Converted 3 test files to `IsolatedAsyncioTestCase` with `aiosqlite`

**Phase 5**: Removed sync `engine` and `SessionLocal` from `app/db.py`; converted startup `create_all` to async

**Alembic**: Untouched — creates its own sync engine via `engine_from_config()`, imports only `DATABASE_URL` and `Base`

## Test plan

- [x] `python -m compileall app tests alembic` — all clean
- [x] Docker build succeeds
- [x] Container starts, all 7 pollers cycle without errors (10 nodes in 3.0s)
- [x] Page navigation dramatically faster — no more 15-20s delays
- [x] All API endpoints return 200
- [x] Alembic `upgrade head` still works
- [ ] Run full test suite in Docker: `docker compose exec smp python -m unittest discover -s tests`

items.title: refactor: migrate from dataclasses to Pydantic models
items.body:
## Summary

This PR refactors the configuration models from Python dataclasses to Pydantic models, providing better validation, clearer error messages, and simplified configuration loading.

## Changes

### Models (`models.py`)
- Replaced all `@dataclass` decorators with Pydantic `BaseModel`
- Converted `__post_init__` validation to `@field_validator` and `@model_validator`
- Updated `field()` to `Field()` for default factories
- Added `model_config` to `ReplicationConfig` to handle schema field shadowing

### Config Manager (`manager.py`)
- Simplified `load_config()` to use deep merge + Pydantic validation
- Removed manual parsing of nested config structures (Pydantic handles this)
- Added `_save_defaults()` function to generate defaults.yaml from Pydantic models
- Added `main()` function to allow regenerating defaults.yaml via CLI
- Added `DefaultsReplicationConfig` to imports

### Tests
- Updated tests to expect `ValidationError` instead of `ValueError` for Pydantic validation
- Added `from pydantic import ValidationError` imports
- Updated error message patterns to match Pydantic's error format

### Dependencies
- Added `pydantic>=2.0.0` to pyproject.toml

## Benefits

1. **Better Validation**: Pydantic provides automatic type checking and validation with clear error messages
2. **Simplified Code**: Reduced manual parsing logic in `load_config()` from ~160 lines to ~80 lines
3. **Type Safety**: Better IDE support and type checking
4. **Defaults Management**: Can now regenerate defaults.yaml from code using `python -m mongo_replication.config.manager`
5. **Deep Merge**: Simplified deep merging of defaults with user configs

## Testing

All 270 unit tests pass:
- ✅ All config model tests
- ✅ All config transformation tests
- ✅ All other unit tests

Run tests with:
```bash
uv run pytest tests/unit/ -v
```

items.title: [codex] migrate Python tooling to uv

labels.name: enhancement
labels.description: New feature or request

items.body:
## Summary
This change migrates the repository from the old single `requirements.txt` workflow to a root `uv` project backed by `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock`, and adds a repo-level `Makefile` to make the common development commands easier to use.

For contributors, the practical effect is that Python setup, test execution, app startup, and script usage now run through `uv` consistently from the repository root. The repo still behaves as one shared Python application spanning the existing backend modules, so the migration improves dependency management and local reproducibility without forcing a risky package split.

## Problem
The repository was still carrying a mixed Python toolchain story: a single `requirements.txt`, shell-default interpreter drift, and docs/scripts that assumed `pip`-style installation or direct `python scripts/...` execution. That made local setup less reproducible and made it too easy to end up on the wrong interpreter or with imports that only worked incidentally from a specific shell context.

At the same time, the project needed a lighter-weight contributor workflow. The new `Makefile` provides short aliases for the commands people are actually using repeatedly, while keeping the underlying `uv` commands explicit and visible.

## Root Cause
The Python side of the monorepo had never been formalized as a root project. It relied on repo-root execution conventions and implicit import behavior instead of an explicit dependency manager and lockfile.

That was especially visible in direct script execution and pytest collection: once the repo moved under `uv`, commands needed a stable way to resolve the repo-root modules (`api`, `agents`, `shared`) from the root project environment.

## Fix
The change introduces a root `pyproject.toml` with Python 3.12 constraints, first-party runtime dependencies, and a dev dependency group, then generates and commits `uv.lock`. The old `requirements.txt` file is removed.

The docs and script/help text were updated to standardize on `uv sync`, `uv run pytest tests`, `uv run python -m uvicorn ...`, and `uv run python scripts/...` commands. A concise `AGENTS.md` was also rewritten to reflect the repo's real toolchain and constraints.

To keep the current monorepo shape working safely, the Python project remains a single shared app rather than being split into multiple Python packages. Tests and direct repo scripts were updated so imports continue to resolve correctly from the root `uv` environment.

The new `Makefile` adds short, repo-friendly commands for setup, tests, local servers, smoke checks, formatting, linting, type checking, and registry sync. `make check` is positioned as the practical passing verification set, while `make lint` and `make typecheck` are available to expose the existing repo-wide backlog.

## Validation
I validated the migration with the commands below:

- `uv sync`
- `uv run pytest tests -q`
- `uv run python scripts/sync_agents_from_registry.py --help`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn api.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8010`
- `uv run python -m uvicorn agents.research.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5002`
- `make help`
- `make check`

## Notes
`make lint` / `ruff` and `make typecheck` / `mypy` are intentionally exposed in the `Makefile`, but they still report pre-existing repo-wide issues and were not treated as merge-blocking for this migration.

items.title: Migrate tasks to Python mise tasks, fix CALLER_PWD
items.body:
## Summary

- **Migrate all 5 task shims to Python mise tasks** — `get`, `set`, `body`, `query`, `init` were bash one-liners that `exec uv run ... farts.py <cmd> "$@"`. Now each is a standalone Python mise task using `#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --script` with PEP 723 inline dependencies. Tasks import library functions directly from `lib/farts.py`.
- **Fix CALLER_PWD path resolution** — relative file paths broke when called via shiv because the working directory changes to the package root. Added `resolve_path()` to the library that prepends `CALLER_PWD` to relative paths.
- **Remove `main()` from lib/farts.py** — the monolithic CLI dispatcher is no longer needed since each task handles its own args via mise's `#USAGE` parsing.
- **Rewrite tests to go through mise** — tests now use a `farts()` wrapper that calls `mise run`, exercising the full pipeline including CALLER_PWD resolution. All paths in tests are relative, catching the exact bug that was fixed.

## Test plan

- [x] `mise run test` — 26/26 tests pass
- [x] Relative paths work via CALLER_PWD (dedicated test)
- [x] Absolute paths still work (dedicated test)
- [x] `init` with field=value args works (shlex parsing of shell-quoted variadic env var)

items.title: feat: migrate DoltgreSQL to Dolt MySQL
items.body:
## Summary

Migrate acme-health from DoltgreSQL (PostgreSQL wire protocol, beta) to Dolt MySQL (MySQL wire protocol, mature). Smoke testing the original DoltgreSQL setup hit three blockers: no SQL isolation levels, missing `EXCLUDED` keyword for UPSERTs, and underdocumented environment variables. Dolt's MySQL wire has been stable since 2019 and has native .NET Aspire integration via `AddMySql`.

17 files changed across 5 commits. All tests pass (1 C# unit + 29 Python).

## Changes

**Phase 1 — AppHost Infrastructure** (`db58af0`)
- `AddContainer("doltgresql")` → `AddMySql("dolt").WithImage("dolthub/dolt-sql-server", "v1.50.2")`
- `AddParameter("dolt-password", secret: true)` with User Secrets for credential management
- `AddDatabase("acme-health")` for auto DB creation
- Manual `ReferenceExpression` replaced with native `WithReference(db)` on all 4 services
- `DOLT_ROOT_HOST=%` for Docker network access (Dolt v1.46+ security)
- Python endpoint fix: `WithEndpoint` replaces `WithHttpEndpoint` (aspire#14408)

**Phase 2 — FhirIngest MySQL Migration** (`b968c22`)
- Npgsql 9.0.3 → MySqlConnector 2.4.0
- `$N` positional params → `@name` named params
- Row alias UPSERT syntax (`AS new ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE col = new.col`)
- Schema DDL: `TEXT PRIMARY KEY` → `VARCHAR(255)`, inline FK → `CONSTRAINT FOREIGN KEY`
- `DOLT_COMMIT` param: `$1` → `@msg`

**Phase 3 — Wearable Normalizer MySQL Migration** (`adf0a9b`)
- psycopg → aiomysql
- `parse_connection_string()` returns `dict` (aiomysql kwargs) instead of DSN string
- aiomysql connection pattern: explicit `connect(**kwargs)` / `commit()` / `close()`
- DDL: `SERIAL` → `INT AUTO_INCREMENT`
- clinical-extractor dependency swapped (stub service, no logic changes)

**Phase 4 — Documentation** (`12eaec4`)
- ADR-1001 superseded with migration rationale (3 blocking DoltgreSQL gaps)
- README architecture diagram, technology table, driver names updated
- Sequence diagrams: participant labels changed

**CR Polish** (`9261b07`)
- Removed hardcoded password from appsettings.json → User Secrets
- Replaced deprecated `VALUES()` with row alias syntax for MySQL 8.0.20+ forward compat
- Restructured `create_schema()` for cleaner connection cleanup
- Added user-secrets setup step to README

## Quality Evidence

| Phase | Review Score | Challenger | Tests |
|-------|-------------|------------|-------|
| 1: AppHost | 9.3/10 | 5 approved, 0 challenges | `dotnet build` clean |
| 2: FhirIngest | 9.1/10 | 10 approved, 0 challenges | 1 unit pass |
| 3: Wearable | 9.3/10 | 12 approved, 0 challenges | 29/29 pytest pass |
| 4: Docs | 9.5/10 | — | grep validation |
| CR Polish | — | — | all tests re-verified |

**CodeRabbit CLI**: 4 findings identified and resolved before PR creation.

## Testing

- [x] `dotnet build AcmeHealth.slnx` — 0 errors, 0 warnings
- [x] `dotnet test --filter "Category!=Integration"` — 1 passed
- [x] `uv run pytest` (wearable-normalizer) — 29 passed
- [ ] End-to-end with Docker — deferred to smoke test (acme-svu.1)

## Review Guidance

| Priority | Files | Why |
|----------|-------|-----|
| High | `src/AppHost/AppHost.cs` | Aspire orchestration — all service wiring |
| High | `src/Acme.Stack.FhirIngest/Program.cs` | Largest code change — SQL dialect, params |
| Medium | `src/services/wearable-normalizer/main.py` | Python driver swap, connection parser |
| Medium | `src/Acme.Stack.Core/Models.cs` | Shared schema DDL |
| Low | `tests/`, `docs/`, `README.md` | Mechanical updates |

## Security

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---------|------------|
| Database credentials | `AddParameter(secret: true)` + `dotnet user-secrets` — no secrets in source |
| Docker network | `DOLT_ROOT_HOST=%` scoped to container network only |

## Spec

Driven by [dolt-mysql-migration](https://github.com/peterkc/acme-sdk/tree/specs/dolt-mysql-migration) — 5 phases, consensus score 9.0/10 from triple-model evaluation.

items.title: fix: migrate to LangChain 1.2.x create_agent API, require Python 3.12+
items.body:
## Summary

Fixes `ImportError: cannot import name 'AgentExecutor' from 'langchain.agents'` when running with LangChain 1.2.x. The legacy `AgentExecutor` + `create_tool_calling_agent` API was removed in LangChain 1.2.x — this PR migrates to the new `create_agent` API (which uses LangGraph under the hood).

**Key changes:**
- **Orchestrator**: Replaced `AgentExecutor`/`create_tool_calling_agent` with `create_agent` from `langchain.agents`. Agent invocation now uses the LangGraph message-based pattern (`{"messages": [...]}`) instead of `{"input": ..., "chat_history": ...}`
- **Dependencies**: `langchain>=0.3.0` → `>=1.2.0`, replaced `langchain-community` with `langgraph>=1.0.0`
- **Python**: Minimum bumped from 3.10 → 3.12 (user requirement for unsloth compatibility); `(str, Enum)` → `StrEnum`
- **Response extraction**: Added `_extract_response()` helper to pull the final `AIMessage` content from the LangGraph result

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Lost `AgentExecutor` guardrails** — `max_iterations`, `handle_parsing_errors`, and `return_intermediate_steps` were all configured on the old `AgentExecutor` but have no equivalent passed to `create_agent`. `config.MAX_AGENT_ITERATIONS` and `self._verbose` are now unused dead code. Verify the agent terminates properly on complex queries and doesn't loop indefinitely with malformed tool calls from `llama3.2`.
- [ ] **`_extract_response()` edge case** — returns `""` if the last message has no content (e.g., tool call without follow-up). Run all CLI commands (`analyze`, `query`, `chat`, `portfolio`, `news`, `market`) and confirm non-empty output.
- [ ] **End-to-end test** — this was NOT tested against a running Ollama server. Run with `ollama serve` + `llama3.2` + `nomic-embed-text` pulled. Suggested test plan:
  1. `ai-agent portfolio` (no LLM needed)
  2. `ai-agent market -s AAPL`
  3. `ai-agent news`
  4. `ai-agent analyze` (full agent loop with tool calling)
  5. `ai-agent chat` (verify multi-turn conversation works)
- [ ] **Chat history ordering change** — `chat()` now appends the `HumanMessage` *before* invoking the agent (previously it was appended *after*). This is arguably more correct but is a behavioral change.

### Notes
- `self._verbose` is set from config but no longer used — `create_agent` doesn't accept a `verbose` parameter. Consider using LangGraph's `debug=True` parameter or removing the field.
- `config.MAX_AGENT_ITERATIONS` is still defined in config but no longer referenced by the orchestrator.

items.title: Add seed_db.py to populate db.sqlite3 with realistic sample data
items.body:
No tooling existed to bootstrap the SQLite database with meaningful data for development/testing. Every model was empty after migrations.

## What's added

**`seed_db.py`** — standalone script, bootstraps Django itself, safe to re-run (fully idempotent via `get_or_create`):

```bash
python manage.py migrate
python seed_db.py
```

## Data seeded

| Model | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `User` | 11 | 1 admin, 2 librarians, 3 teachers, 5 students |
| `Category` / `subCategory` | 8 / 32 | Fiction, Science, Tech, Philosophy… |
| `Author` / `Tag` | 22 / 18 | Real names (Tolstoy, Navoiy, Hawking…) |
| `Kitob` | 20 | Real titles, ISBNs, shelf locations, page counts |
| `Journals` | 5 | Nature, IEEE Spectrum, Zamin ilmi… |
| `Reservation` | 19 | Mix of `pending` / `given` / `returned` |
| `Rating` + `Comment` | 16 pairs | 1–5 star scores with text |
| `Bookmark` | 23 | |
| `Notification` | 10 | |
| `News` | 5 | |

## Key implementation notes

- Reservations are created as `pending` then status-updated via direct `QuerySet.update()` to bypass the complex signal chain — avoiding unintended side-effects (quantity mutations, auto-approve loops) when inserting historical data.
- Ratings trigger the `set_avg_rating` signal automatically, so `Kitob.rating` is populated correctly after seeding.
- Credentials are printed at exit for convenience.

items.title: Migrate models to nn.Module, add pluggable learning rules, remove sys.path hacks, add type hints
items.body: ## Summary Four major improvements to the Arthedain codebase: ### 1. Migrate models to `torch.nn.Module` All core model classes now inherit from `nn.Module`: - **LIFLayer**: `v` and `refractory_counter` registered as buffers; `forward()` added as alias for `step()` - **RSNN**: `W_in` and `W_rec` stored as `nn.Parameter(requires_grad=False)`; `prev_spikes` as buffer - **Readout**: `W` and `b` as `nn.Parameter(requires_grad=False)`; `_smooth_state` as buffer - **DualHebbian / DualHebbianAccumulator**: eligibility traces as buffers; `MultiLayerHebbian` uses `nn.ModuleList` This unlocks `.to(device)`, `state_dict()` / `load_state_dict()`, and `parameters()` across all models. ### 2. Pluggable learning rules in `OnlineTrainer` New `TrainerConfig.recurrent_rule` field selects the recurrent weight update rule: - `"hebbian"` — standard `lr * E` (default, backward compatible) - `"rstdp"` — reward-modulated STDP with configurable baseline - `"bcm"` — BCM rule with sliding activity threshold - `"oja"` — Oja's rule for self-normalizing weights ### 3. Remove `sys.path` hacks Removed `sys.path.insert(0, ...)` / `sys.path.append(...)` from **15 files** across tests, experiments, examples, and hardware. All imports now use proper package paths (`from models.rsnn import RSNN`, etc.). Added `pythonpath = ["."]` to `pyproject.toml` for pytest discovery. ### 4. Complete type hints All model files (`lif.py`, `rsnn.py`, `readout.py`, `hebbian.py`) and training files (`online_trainer.py`, `update_rules.py`) now have full type annotations on every function signature and return type. **Tests**: 77 passing (up from 64) — added nn.Module integration tests and pluggable rule tests. Ruff lint passes clean. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] Verify `state_dict()` / `load_state_dict()` roundtrip works for your saved checkpoints (the tensor keys changed from raw attributes to registered parameters/buffers) - [ ] If you have custom code that assigns directly to `rsnn.W_rec = ...`, update it to use `rsnn.W_rec.data.copy_(...)` instead (nn.Parameter requires `.data` for in-place mutation) - [ ] Test the new learning rules: `TrainerConfig(recurrent_rule="rstdp")` with `trainer.step(x, reward=1.0)` in reward mode - [ ] Run `python -m pytest tests/ -v` to confirm all 77 tests pass ### Notes - Backward compatibility is maintained for all existing APIs — `RSNN(input_size, hidden_size)` still works identically - The `core/model_utils.py` checkpoint loading now uses `.data.copy_()` for nn.Parameter compatibility - `examples/debug_learning.py` weight normalization updated to use `.data.div_()` instead of direct assignment Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/29f9e0709897423ea1029fa266a06bf5 Requested by: @Aidistides ---

items.title: chore(infra): setup Docker PostgreSQL and secure environment variables
items.body:
## 🎯 Context
To ensure high scalability and consistency across different environments (preventing the "it works on my machine" issue), the project needed a robust database infrastructure. We transitioned from the default SQLite database to a containerized PostgreSQL environment using Docker. Additionally, sensitive credentials needed to be isolated from the source code.

## 🛠️ What was done
* Created a docker-compose.yml file to spin up a PostgreSQL 15 database container.
* configured python-decouple to manage environment variables securely.
* Updated settings.py to connect to the Dockerized database using variables from a local .env file.
* Ensured the .env file is properly ignored by Git via .gitignore.
* Ran initial migrations successfully into the new database.

## 🧪 How to test
1. Switch this branch: git switch feature/setup-docker-postgres
2. Create a .env file in the root directory containing SECRET_KEY and POSTGRES_PASSWORD.
3. Start the database container: docker-compose up -d
4. Apply migrations: python manage.py migrate
5. Create a superuser: python manage.py createsuperuser
6. Run the server (python manage.py runserver) and log into the /admin panel to verify the catalog tables are accessible.

## ✅ Checklist
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code.
- [x] Migrations were successfully generated and tested locally.
- [x] Environment variables are hidden and not committed.
- [x] Docker container spins up without errors.
- [x] Django successfully connects to PostgreSQL.

items.title: Refactors to modular package with enhanced PHI controls

Adds granular transport and proxy options for ClinicalTrials.gov queries to ensure PHI-safe retrieval, with POST and proxy routing based on query content. Enforces fail-closed consent checks for external LLM usage with clinical data. Updates documentation to clarify security/operational controls, dependency setup, and containerized workflows.

items.title: Migrate to pyproject.toml and add headless CMS JSON API
items.body:
Replaces the legacy `setup.py` with `pyproject.toml` (PEP 517/518/621) and exposes the `Page` model via a REST-style JSON API to enable headless CMS usage.

## Packaging
- `pyproject.toml` replaces `setup.py` with `setuptools>=61.0` build backend
- Correct Python classifiers: drops Python 2, adds 3.8–3.12 and Django 4.0–4.2
- Fixes license classifier (was incorrectly `BSD`, now `LGPLv3`)
- Bumps `django` requirement from `>=3.0.7` to `>=4.0`
- Adds `manage.py` + `test_settings.py` for the test suite

## Headless CMS API

New endpoints served under `feedly/urls.py`:

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/` | Blocks health check |
| GET | `/pages/` | List all pages |
| POST | `/pages/` | Create page (auth required) |
| GET | `/pages/<id>/` | Retrieve page |
| PUT | `/pages/<id>/` | Update page (owner only) |
| DELETE | `/pages/<id>/` | Delete page (owner only) |

```python
# Example: list pages
GET /feedly/pages/
# → {"pages": [{"id": 1, "name": "My Page", "content": "...", "date": "2024-..."}, ...]}

# Example: create a page (authenticated)
POST /feedly/pages/
{"name": "About", "content": "Hello world"}
# → 201 {"id": 2, "name": "About", "content": "Hello world", "date": "..."}
```

## Bug Fixes
- `feedly/apps.py`: `name` was `'feedly.feedly'` (no such subpackage), corrected to `'feedly'`
- `feedly/__init__.py`: removes stale `default_app_config` (deprecated Django 3.2+)
- `feedly/urls.py`: removes `from django.conf.urls import url` (removed in Django 4.0); switches to explicit view imports
- `feedly/views.py`: removes broken imports from non-existent `.feed` and `.core` modules

- Add Kubernetes Job manifest to run migrations inside minikube
- Verify end-to-end Python-to-Postgres connectivity from inside the cluster

## What changed and why

### `migrations/001_create_jobs.sql` (new)

Creates the `jobs` table with 16 columns and 3 indexes. Every column maps 1:1 from the `Job` dataclass in `models.py`. Key design decisions:

- **`UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()`** — UUIDs avoid sequential-ID guessing and work across distributed producers. `gen_random_uuid()` is built into Postgres 13+, no extension needed.
- **`idempotency_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE`** — prevents duplicate job submission at the DB level.
- **`JSONB`** for `payload` / `result_payload` — binary format, supports indexing, faster reads than plain JSON.
- **`TIMESTAMPTZ`** for all timestamps — timezone-aware prevents bugs when pods run in different TZ configs.
- **`status TEXT`** not `ENUM` — easier to evolve without a migration; valid values enforced in application code.
- **`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` / `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS`** throughout — makes the migration idempotent (safe to re-run).

**The 3 indexes:**

1. **`idx_jobs_dequeue`** — `(priority DESC, created_at ASC) WHERE status = 'queued'`
   - A **partial index** that only contains queued rows. Columns match the `ORDER BY` of the worker's `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` dequeue query exactly.
   - As most jobs move to `completed`/`dead_lettered`, this index stays small. Postgres can do an index-only scan for the dequeue query, which is the hottest path in the system.

2. **`idx_jobs_lease`** — `(lease_expires_at) WHERE status = 'running' AND lease_expires_at IS NOT NULL`
   - A **partial index** covering only actively-running jobs with a lease.
   - The lease reaper queries `WHERE status = 'running' AND lease_expires_at < now()`. This index makes that query fast and stays small since only in-flight jobs are indexed.

3. **`idx_jobs_type_status`** — `(job_type, status)`
   - Standard composite btree index for admin queries like "show all failed email jobs".
   - `job_type` first because it has higher cardinality and is the more common filter prefix.

### `src/taskqueue/migrate.py` (new)

Reads all `*.sql` files from `migrations/` in sorted order and executes them against the database. Uses the existing `get_connection()` from `db.py`. The migration directory is resolved via the `MIGRATIONS_DIR` env var, falling back to a relative `migrations/` path (which resolves from the Dockerfile's `WORKDIR /app`).

**Why not Alembic?** The project is in Week 1 with a single table. A simple script that runs `.sql` files in sorted order is the right abstraction level. Can be replaced with Alembic later when the schema evolves.

**Output when run:**
```
Running 001_create_jobs.sql ...
All migrations applied successfully.
```

### `entrypoint.sh` (modified)

Added `migrate)` case to dispatch to `python -m taskqueue.migrate`. This lets Kubernetes Jobs (and developers) run migrations by setting `ROLE=migrate`.

### `Dockerfile` (modified)

Added `COPY migrations/ migrations/` so SQL files are available inside the container. Without this, `migrate.py`'s path resolution would find an empty/missing directory.

### `k8s/migrate-job.yaml` (new)

Kubernetes **Job** (not Deployment) that runs the migration once and exits. Job is the correct K8s primitive for one-shot tasks.

Key settings:
- `imagePullPolicy: Never` — uses the image built directly in minikube's Docker daemon, doesn't try to pull from Docker Hub
- `restartPolicy: Never` + `backoffLimit: 3` — retries up to 3 times on failure (handles the race if migration starts before Postgres is ready), then stops
- `DATABASE_URL` injected via `secretKeyRef` from `postgres-secret` — keeps credentials centralized

### `README.md` (modified)

Added "Build and deploy the task queue" section and updated the project structure listing.

## How to test on your Mac

### 1. Start minikube
```bash
minikube start
```
Starts the cluster (or resumes it if stopped). On first run, add `--driver=docker --memory=4096 --cpus=2`.

### 2. Build the Docker image inside minikube's Docker
```bash
eval $(minikube docker-env)
docker build -t taskqueue:latest .
```
`eval $(minikube docker-env)` redirects your shell's `docker` command to minikube's internal Docker daemon. This puts the image directly where Kubernetes can find it — no registry needed. Every new terminal session needs this again.

### 3. Deploy everything
```bash
kubectl delete job taskqueue-migrate --ignore-not-found
kubectl apply -f k8s/
```
Deploys Postgres (secret, PVC, deployment, service) and the migration Job all at once. The `delete` first is needed because K8s Jobs can't be re-applied without removing the previous one; `--ignore-not-found` prevents errors on first run.

### 4. Wait for Postgres, then check migration
```bash
kubectl get pods -l app=postgres -w
# Wait until you see 1/1 Running, then Ctrl+C

kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/taskqueue-migrate --timeout=60s
kubectl logs job/taskqueue-migrate
```
Expected output:
```
Running 001_create_jobs.sql ...
All migrations applied successfully.
```

### 5. Verify the table and indexes exist
```bash
kubectl run pg-verify --rm -it --restart=Never \
  --image=postgres:16 \
  --env="PGPASSWORD=taskqueue-dev-password" \
  -- psql -h postgres -U taskqueue -d taskqueue --pset pager=off \
  -c "SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='jobs' ORDER BY ordinal_position;"
```
Expected: a table listing all 16 columns with their Postgres types.

```bash
kubectl run pg-idx --rm -it --restart=Never \
  --image=postgres:16 \
  --env="PGPASSWORD=taskqueue-dev-password" \
  -- psql -h postgres -U taskqueue -d taskqueue --pset pager=off \
  -c "SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='jobs';"
```
Expected: 5 indexes — `jobs_pkey`, `jobs_idempotency_key_key`, `idx_jobs_dequeue`, `idx_jobs_lease`, `idx_jobs_type_status`

### 6. Verify Python-to-Postgres connection end-to-end
```bash
kubectl run py-test --rm -it --restart=Never \
  --image=taskqueue:latest \
  --image-pull-policy=Never \
  --env="DATABASE_URL=postgresql://taskqueue:taskqueue-dev-password@postgres:5432/taskqueue" \
  --command -- python -c "
from taskqueue.db import get_connection
conn = get_connection()
cur = conn.execute('SELECT count(*) FROM jobs')
print(f'Row count: {cur.fetchone()[0]}')
conn.close()
print('Connection verified.')
"
```
**Important:** The `--image-pull-policy=Never` flag is required so Kubernetes uses the locally built image instead of trying to pull from Docker Hub (which will fail).

This proves the full chain: Python code → psycopg → `DATABASE_URL` env var → Postgres service DNS → Postgres pod → `jobs` table.

Expected output:
```
Row count: 0
Connection verified.
```

### 7. Re-run migration to confirm idempotency
```bash
kubectl delete job taskqueue-migrate --ignore-not-found
kubectl apply -f k8s/migrate-job.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/taskqueue-migrate --timeout=60s
kubectl logs job/taskqueue-migrate
```
Expected: same success output — `IF NOT EXISTS` clauses make it a no-op.

## Idempotency

The migration uses `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` and `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` throughout, so it is safe to run multiple times. Re-running the migration job is a no-op if the table already exists.

items.title: Switch to self-hosted TrueNAS Scale architecture
items.body:
This PR shifts the project from a Vercel-hosted deployment model to a fully self-hosted Docker Compose setup designed for TrueNAS Scale.

### Changes Included:
1. **Architecture shift:** The Vue 3 SPA is now statically compiled during a multi-stage Docker build and served by the FastAPI backend on a single port (`8000`). This eliminates the need for separate frontend hosting like Vercel or Render.
2. **Backend Proxying & Database:** External calls to Overpass and OSRM have been migrated from the frontend to the Python backend to avoid client rate-limiting and allow storing results locally in a PostGIS database.
3. **Docker Compose Setup:** A complete `docker-compose.yml` is provided alongside a `.env.example` file. It orchestrates the API server, a PostGIS database, Redis, and Valhalla routing.
4. **Database Configuration:** Uses `postgresql+asyncpg` for asynchronous database connections. Lifecycle events handle creating database metadata on startup.

items.title: Fix crash during web directory initialization on Windows Python 3.10
items.body:
Crash on startup after `reflex run` on Windows Python 3.10 (Windows Store), failing during "Initializing the web directory" with a truncated traceback. Two root causes identified and fixed.

## Changes

- **Replace deprecated `rx.Base` with `pydantic.BaseModel` in `Channel`**
  - `rx.Base` emits `DeprecationWarning` on every module import since reflex 0.8.15; Windows Store Python 3.10 can treat this as a hard error during state processing
  - Added explicit `= None` default for `logo` (required by pydantic v2 for optional fields)

- **Fix `meta.json` path resolution**
  - `open("StepDaddyLiveHD/meta.json")` is relative to CWD and fails when the app module is imported from a subprocess (e.g., granian's `ProcessPoolExecutor` compile step on Windows)
  - Changed to `Path(__file__).parent / "meta.json"` for reliable resolution

- **Update `reflex` to `0.8.28`** (latest stable; `0.8.27` already emitted an upgrade warning on startup)

```python
# Before
class Channel(rx.Base):  # DeprecationWarning on every import
    logo: str | None      # Missing default, pydantic v2 issue

with open("StepDaddyLiveHD/meta.json", "r") as f:  # CWD-relative, fragile

# After
class Channel(BaseModel):
    logo: str | None = None

with open(Path(__file__).parent / "meta.json", "r") as f:
```

items.title: feat: Next.js 15 + FastAPI web redesign (Figma design 1:1)
items.body:
## Summary

- Migrates UI from PySide6 to **Next.js 15 + Tauri 2 + Python FastAPI**, matching the Figma Make design pixel-perfect
- 3-panel layout: dark sidebar (`#1a1f36`), Kanban board with drag-and-drop (`@dnd-kit`), AI chat panel with SSE streaming
- Framer Motion animations, Lucide icons, Tailwind CSS — all matching the Figma color spec exactly
- Python `core/` backend (tasks_manager, file_tools, models) preserved unchanged; `tasks.json` format 100% compatible

## Key Technical Details

- **SSR fix**: `page.tsx` → `ClientShell.tsx` (`"use client"` + `dynamic(..., {ssr:false})`) → `AppClient.tsx` to prevent browser APIs running server-side
- **Next.js 15.3.0 bug**: `DevOverlay` accessed `localStorage` during SSR — downgraded to `15.2.4`
- **FastAPI CORS**: `allow_origins=["*"]` with `allow_credentials=False` for browser compatibility
- **SSE confirm flow**: `asyncio.Event` pattern — stream pauses via `await event.wait()`, user approves in frontend, event is set

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run dev` starts on any port without 500 errors
- [ ] Open project via sidebar → Kanban populates from `tasks.json`
- [ ] Settings dialog auto-opens on first run (no API key)
- [ ] Task cards show correct priority badges and can be dragged between columns
- [ ] Chat panel sends messages and streams SSE responses from FastAPI

items.title: Migrate from OpenAI (LangChain ChatOpenAI) to Google Gemini API
items.body:
Replaces the OpenAI/LangChain dependency with Google's `google-generativeai` SDK (`gemini-1.5-flash`), fixes cross-platform path issues in `Main.py`, and aligns env var naming and docs.

## Core changes

- **`Utils/Agents.py`** — swap `ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-5")` for `genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-flash")`; reads `GEMINI_API_KEY` from env; `run()` calls `model.generate_content(prompt)` and returns `response.text`

```python
# Before
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
self.model = ChatOpenAI(temperature=0, model="gpt-5")
response = self.model.invoke(prompt)
return response.content

# After
import google.generativeai as genai
genai.configure(api_key=os.environ["GEMINI_API_KEY"])
self.model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-1.5-flash")
response = self.model.generate_content(prompt)
return response.text
```

- **`Main.py`** — replace Windows-only backslash path with `pathlib.Path`; fix output dir from `results/` → `Results/` (case-sensitive on Linux); remove duplicate `import dotenv`
- **`requirements.txt`** — drop `langchain-openai` and redundant `dotenv`; add `google-generativeai`
- **`apikey.env`** — `OPENAI_API_KEY = ""` → `GEMINI_API_KEY=` (corrects dotenv format: no spaces around `=`)
- **`README.md`** — update setup steps, API key instructions (pointing to https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey), and model references

items.title: Fix Vercel Django deployment runtime
items.body:
### Motivation
- Vercel's legacy `builds`/`routes` configuration caused a runtime error and didn't run Django migrations during deploy. 
- The intent is to adapt the project to the current Vercel Python Runtime and ensure `makemigrations`/`migrate` run automatically on deploy. 

### Description
- Replace the old `builds`/`routes` config with a modern `vercel.json` that uses `buildCommand` to run `python manage.py makemigrations && python manage.py migrate && python manage.py collectstatic --noinput` and configures a `functions`/`rewrites` entry for `wsgi.py`. 
- Add a top-level `wsgi.py` that re-exports the Django WSGI app from `config/wsgi.py`. 
- Update `config/wsgi.py` to expose the WSGI application as `app` (and keep `application = app`) so the runtime can import the expected symbol. 
- Add `.python-version` pinned to `3.12`, create an empty `static/.gitkeep` so `collectstatic` has a source path, and document the deploy behavior in `README.md`. 

### Testing
- `python -m json.tool vercel.json` succeeded and validated `vercel.json`. 
- `python -m py_compile config/wsgi.py wsgi.py` succeeded and validated Python syntax for the modified files. 
- `python manage.py check` failed in this environment because Django is not installed and `pip install -r requirements.txt` could not reach PyPI through the proxy, so full Django checks could not be run here.

------

items.title: Switch to SMB-only mode, add smbclient install helper and UI/UX improvements (v0.3.5)
items.body:
### Motivation

- Simplify and standardize the plugin on SMB transfers only and avoid supporting two transfer modes to reduce complexity and user errors.
- Provide a better onboarding experience by detecting `smbclient`, suggesting install commands and offering an automatic install attempt from the UI when possible.
- Improve the settings UI visibility and the log panel contrast/readability for a clearer user experience.
- Bump plugin version and document the recent fixes in the README changelog.

### Description

- Enforced SMB-only behavior by setting the default `transfer_mode` to `smbclient`, migrating existing settings with `_force_smb_mode()`, and centralizing transfer selection via `_get_transfer_mode()`.
- Reworked connection logic to assume SMB, improved `_test_connection()` error messaging, and added API fields `smbclient_installed` and `smbclient_install_hint` to surface install status and hints.
- Added an `install_smbclient` API command and `_install_smbclient()` implementation that attempts package install across common package managers (with `sudo` if available) and returns structured results.
- Updated the settings UI (`nasbackup_settings.jinja2` and `nasbackup.js`) to remove local-path options, show SMB-only messaging, provide an install button, surface install/test results, and reorganize schedule controls and status behavior.
- Improved CSS (`nasbackup.css`) for better log panel contrast and adjusted log/error/warning colors for readability.
- Updated README changelog and bumped plugin version to `0.3.5` in `__init__.py` and `setup.py`.

### Testing

- Built the package with `python setup.py sdist` which completed successfully.
- Performed a basic import check with `python -c "import octoprint_nasbackup"` which succeeded.
- No bundled unit tests are included in the repository, so no automated test suite was executed.

------

items.title: Build GitHub Actions infrastructure for cross-repo Hats reviews and agentic task execution
items.body:
Hat Stack was spec-only. This makes it operational — runnable in GitHub Actions as a service other repos can hook into, with a CLI for local agents (e.g., Copilot in VS Code) to dispatch both reviews and generative tasks against the Ollama Cloud model pool.

## Review mode — 18-hat PR analysis pipeline

- **`hats-review.yml`** — reusable `workflow_call` other repos reference directly; accepts diffs via inline content or artifact-based flow (`diff_artifact` input) for cross-workflow compatibility
- **`hats-dispatch.yml`** — `repository_dispatch` handler for API-triggered reviews with callback to source PR/issue
- **`hats-self-review.yml`** — self-review on PRs to this repo
- **`.github/actions/run-hats/action.yml`** — composite action for inline use in any workflow, uses `$GITHUB_ACTION_PATH` so forks run their own code
- **`scripts/hats_runner.py`** — Conductor implementation: hat selection by diff triggers, tiered-parallel execution, cost budget gate (G1), security fast-path gate (G2), consolidation, Gold Hat/CoVE adjudication, risk score computation per CATALOG.md formula

## Task mode — agentic work execution

- **`hats-task.yml`** — `repository_dispatch` + `workflow_dispatch` for generative tasks
- **`scripts/hats_task_runner.py`** — 6 task types (`generate_code`, `generate_docs`, `refactor`, `analyze`, `plan`, `test`), each mapped to optimal hat with tier-aware model selection (Tier 1 tasks get Tier 1 models like glm-5.1, others use hat-assigned primaries). Primary hat generates, supporting hats review/enhance, Gold Hat does final QA.
- **`scripts/hat`** — Bash CLI wrapping `gh api` dispatches with auto-detection of hat_stack repo from git remote. Local agent runs `hat task generate_code "Build auth module" --repo org/app --pr 42`, results post back as PR comment.

```bash
# Review a diff
git diff main | hat review - --repo myorg/app --pr 123

# Generate code via hat expertise
hat task generate_code "Build FastAPI auth with JWT" --repo myorg/app --pr 42

# Plan a migration
hat task plan "Migrate REST to GraphQL" --repo myorg/app --issue 10
```

## Fork-friendly design

- **No hardcoded repo references** — reusable workflow uses `${{ github.repository }}` for checkout, composite action uses `$GITHUB_ACTION_PATH`, CLI auto-detects from git remote
- Secrets never in code — all via `${{ secrets.* }}` / `os.environ`
- `.env.example` template, `FORK_SETUP.md` (fork → add one secret → done)
- `.gitignore` hardened for credential files, runtime outputs
- Preflight health-check in runners with actionable error messages

## Configuration

- **`scripts/hat_configs.yml`** — all 18 hats with model assignments per Implementation Guide §E2.2, personas, triggers, gate config, risk score weights. 8 models across 4 tiers (glm-5.1, kimi-k2.5, deepseek-v3.1, minimax-m2.7, nemotron-3-super, qwen3-coder, nemotron-3-nano, ministral-3).

## Security hardening

- **Shell word-splitting prevention** — all workflows use bash arrays (`"${ARGS[@]}"`) instead of string concatenation for CLI arg construction
- **Python code injection prevention** — prompts passed via environment variables instead of interpolation into inline Python literals
- **PR title injection prevention** — `hats-self-review.yml` sanitizes PR title via env var and `tr` before use in shell commands
- **GitHub Actions output injection** — newline sanitization on all `repository_dispatch` payload values written to `GITHUB_OUTPUT`
- **Null-safe artifact handling** — `find -print0` pattern for safe filename handling in artifact downloads
- **Shell injection in `hat` CLI** — variables passed via `os.environ` instead of string interpolation

items.title: Migrate to llama-server and enhance AI capability handling
items.body:
This pull request introduces robust AI capability checks and error handling throughout the file organizing pipeline, ensuring that the backend gracefully handles cases where AI services (such as LLM or embeddings) are unavailable. It also updates documentation and environment configuration to reflect the switch from in-process `llama-cpp-python` to an external `llama-server` for LLM inference. The changes are grouped below by theme.

**AI Capability Checks and Error Handling**

* Added `_collect_organize_ai_errors` and `_build_ai_unavailable_result` helper functions to centralize AI availability checks and error reporting. These functions ensure that any AI-related failures are consistently logged and surfaced in API responses.
* Integrated live AI capability checks at multiple steps in the organize pipeline (classification, summary, rename, and final classification), returning a structured error response if any AI service is unavailable. This prevents partial or misleading results when AI is down. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR704-R722) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR733-R746) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL681-R814) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR835-R848) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR874-R887) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR939-R952)
* Updated pipeline step numbering and ensured that AI status and timings are tracked in the pipeline metadata for each file processed. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR537) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL692-R825) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL718-R865) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL743-R904) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL764-R925) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL796-R971)

**Service and Import Refactoring**

* Refactored service imports to clarify module boundaries, moving AI-related services under `app.services.ai` and file-related services under `app.services.files`. This improves code organization and maintainability. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL51-R61) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR36-R39)

**Documentation and Environment Updates**

* Updated `.env.example` to remove in-process llama-cpp settings and add external `llama-server` configuration, reflecting the new runtime architecture.
* Revised `README.md` to explain the use of `llama-server`, update model instructions, clarify environment variables, and remove references to deprecated endpoints and files. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R6-R14) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L22-R26) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L36-R54) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L81-R86) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L104-R114) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L134)

**Deprecation and Cleanup**

* Removed references to the deprecated `init_base_path.py` endpoint and related documentation, aligning with the new organize flow.

**Pipeline Robustness**

* Ensured that AI capability errors are surfaced early and consistently, preventing downstream failures and improving reliability for users when AI services are unavailable. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR181-R254) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR704-R722) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR733-R746) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cL681-R814) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR835-R848) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR874-R887) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-1ab307128b21cc4aad182f8b37611b1f6a2ed3c431bf0fdbdfac3d79d1afa50cR939-R952)

items.title: Fix Python 3.11/3.12 compatibility: update stale deps, fix install docs, repair SKU bug
items.body:
Pinned dependencies (`numpy==1.24.3`, `pandas==2.0.1`, stale Azure SDK pins) blocked installation on Python 3.11+ and caused silent runtime failures. Install instructions also pointed to the wrong `requirements.txt` path.

## `requirements.txt`
- `numpy==1.24.3` → `>=1.26.0,<3.0.0` (1.24.x tops out at Python 3.11)
- `pandas==2.0.1` → `>=2.1.0,<3.0.0` (2.0.x tops out at Python 3.11)
- `azure-core==1.26.4` → `>=1.32.0,<2.0.0`
- `azure-identity==1.12.0` → `>=1.19.0,<2.0.0`
- `azure-mgmt-core>=1.5.0` → `>=1.6.0,<2.0.0`

## `pullAzureCacheForRedisStats.py`
- **Repaired silent SKU bug**: missing comma between `'ComputeOptimized_X20'` and `'ComputeOptimized_X50'` caused Python to silently concatenate them into a single string, making `ComputeOptimized_X50` unreachable in AMR SKU lookups
- Removed unused imports (`AzureCliCredential`, `os`) and unused `tenant_id` variable

## Docs (`README.md`, `inventory/python/README.md`)
- Fixed install command: `pip install -r ../../requirements.txt` → `python -m pip install -r requirements.txt` (run from repo root)
- Updated stated Python requirement: `3.8+` → `3.11+`
- Added explicit `cd inventory/python` step before running the script

items.title: Refactor StructureAnalyzer to use iterative stack-based traversals
items.body:
This pull request addresses reliability and performance bottlenecks in `StructureAnalyzer` by migrating recursive file system traversals to stack-based iterative algorithms.

**Improvements:**
1. **Prevent Recursion Errors**: Deeply nested directories no longer risk hitting Python's recursion limit.
2. **Crash Prevention**: Added `try...except PermissionError` blocks when iterating over directories, preventing the entire analysis from crashing due to a single unreadable folder.
3. **Memory/Performance**: Iterative traversals generally incur less overhead than deeply nested function calls in Python.

The changes preserve the exact output structure and behavior (including the dictionary insertion order in `_build_tree`) while significantly hardening the analyzer against real-world repository anomalies.

items.title: Migrate to psycopg 3.x for Python 3.13 compatibility
items.body:
`psycopg2-binary` lacks Python 3.13 support, breaking Streamlit Cloud deployments.

## Changes

**Dependencies**
- `requirements.txt`: Replace `psycopg2-binary==2.9.9` → `psycopg[binary]==3.1.18`

**SQLAlchemy Dialect**
- `backend/app/core/config.py`: Update connection URL to use explicit driver dialect
```python
# Before
return f"postgresql://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."

# After  
return f"postgresql+psycopg://{self.POSTGRES_USER}:..."
```

**Import Path**
- `backend/app/db/database.py`: Fix relative import `from .config` → `from ..core.config`

**Docker Images**
- `deployment/docker/Dockerfile.{backend,frontend}`: Bump base image `python:3.11-slim` → `python:3.13-slim`

**Documentation**
- `README.md`: Note Python 3.13 support, update setup instructions

## Compatibility

Maintains backward compatibility with Python 3.11+. No API changes.

items.title: Fix Django admin static files and login 500 on Vercel
items.body:
Django admin renders unstyled on Vercel because `collectstatic` output (`staticfiles/`) is never bundled into the Lambda — WhiteNoise has nothing to serve. Additionally, `migrate` never ran on deploy, causing a 500 on `POST /api/auth/login/` due to missing `authtoken_token` table.

## Changes

- **`vercel.json`** — Add `"includeFiles": "staticfiles/**"` to the `@vercel/python` build config so collected assets are packaged into the Lambda; bump `maxLambdaSize` from 15 mb → 30 mb to accommodate them.

- **`build_files.sh`** — Add `python manage.py migrate --noinput` before `collectstatic`; without this, the auth token table never exists in production.

- **`settings.py`** — Switch `STATICFILES_STORAGE` from `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage` to `CompressedStaticFilesStorage`. The manifest variant requires `staticfiles.json` to be present and valid at startup; on serverless this is fragile. The non-manifest variant resolves files by name directly.

- **`backend/README.md`** — Document the static file strategy and verification steps:
  ```bash
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/admin/css/base.css
  curl -I https://<domain>/static/jazzmin/css/main.css
  # both should return HTTP 200
  ```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Goal
Fix Django admin UI styling on Vercel for `vansh-bit/pantry-pal` backend deployment. Admin page renders without CSS/JS (unstyled), which indicates static files are not being served correctly.

## Context
- Backend deployed on Vercel with Root Directory = `backend`.
- Django uses WhiteNoise and `CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage`.
- `backend/build.sh` runs `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `python manage.py migrate`, `python manage.py collectstatic --noinput`.
- Current `backend/vercel.json` routes `/static/(.*)`, `/admin/(.*)`, and `/(.*)` to `pantry_pal/wsgi.py`.
- Admin remains unstyled in production.

## Requirements
Implement a robust static files strategy for Vercel so `/static/admin/...` and `/static/jazzmin/...` assets return 200 and admin renders correctly.

## Tasks
1. Update Django static settings to work reliably on Vercel serverless:
   - Ensure `STATIC_ROOT` is set to a directory that is present at runtime.
   - Prefer collecting into a directory that Vercel will serve as static output.
   - Consider using `STATICFILES_DIRS` if needed.
   - Ensure WhiteNoise settings are correct.

2. Update Vercel configuration so static files are served as real static assets:
   - In `backend/vercel.json`, add routes to serve static files from the collected directory before routing to WSGI.
   - If needed, use Vercel's `handle: "filesystem"` and/or explicit `src` to static directory.

3. Update build step to collect static into the chosen directory.

4. Add a quick doc snippet (e.g., in backend README) describing how static works on Vercel and how to verify with:
   - `curl -I /static/admin/css/base.css`
   - `curl -I /static/jazzmin/css/main.css`

## Acceptance criteria
- After deploying, requests to:
  - `/static/admin/css/base.css`
  - `/static/jazzmin/css/main.css`
  return HTTP 200.
- `/admin/` renders with proper CSS.

## Notes
Keep changes minimal and safe. Ensure existing API routes continue to work. If changing `STATIC_ROOT`, ensure it doesn't break local development.

items.body:
Bumps [protobuf](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf) from 3.19.3 to 5.29.6.
Release notes

Sourced from protobuf's releases.

Protocol Buffers v34.0-rc1

Announcements

  • This version includes breaking changes to: C++, Objective-C, PHP, Python.
  • [Bazel] Remove deprecated ProtoInfo.transitive_imports. Use equivalent transitive_sources instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/0a5c2f6b633c1e5259f566cb42d30fe347b8aadb)
  • [C++] Make generator headers private (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3a2af3510f0d454dbe3e4dc281674b61c4d20b9e)
  • [C++] Add a debug check that the target of CopyFrom is not a descendant of the source. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/7a7589823d2cfaaf7994b050e98d5d553bc9b1c1)
  • [C++] Add [[nodiscard]] to many APIs. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/a70115f33f9af2c4b2202c800b84837e7fe0d738)
  • [C++] Make the arena-enabled constructors of RepeatedField, RepeatedPtrField, and Map private. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ef890c3d0c79398c70e047fe5dd893f460ba2336)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::label() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or is_required() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b76faa921fdd244f374c7be0bddd4050fc42c292)
  • [C++] Removes proto2::util::MessageDifferencer::AddIgnoreCriteria that takes a raw pointer as an argument in favor of the overload that takes a unique_ptr. Remove macro PROTOBUF_FUTURE_REMOVE_ADD_IGNORE_CRITERIA (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b115358c64127896fed88b8b5ef5d91d86d8cbae)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::has_optional_keyword() in OSS. Use is_repeated() or has_presence() instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/68346ec9348e932664e58c3ecdcd1478f95233a8)
  • [C++] Remove AddUnusedImportTrackFile() and ClearUnusedImportTrackFiles(). Remove PROTOBUF_FUTURE_RENAME_ADD_UNUSED_IMPORT (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/837a2cd1d6c75402b2503ffe7cd8aeaf25868536)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated FieldDescriptor::is_optional() in OSS. Use (!is_required() && !is_repeated()) instead (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9dbc5d479a8e453921485d8d3de47fb3c005f1af)
  • [C++] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • [C++] All entity names have length limit (2afb0dc)
  • [ObjC] Remove generate_minimal_imports generation option warning (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/45b1297fdaad5a9436d0e207422168c38dc45ac4)
  • [ObjC] Fix nullability annotations on some GPB*Dictionary types. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ea67d6d26a48478a567c404679e3bb99cf230d50)
  • [ObjC] Remove -[GPBFieldDescriptor optional] (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/3414dc151eb4dcbdb2ca952e2589993bf7af75c4)
  • [Other] Remove deprecated flag for enabling MSVC support (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/97c979be6e0907e1051bee62584dac4594e73fa7)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/9c45014099a4f7004fab6dd1278de2f4f2a393c5)
  • [PHP] Remove deprecated PHP APIs FieldDescriptor getLabel, use IsRepeated or isRequired instead. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cd76e675b14d00dda5623b30835d2bc7105fccc6, https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/42081219920c6fad17ba6ddd1e28d111bcfb3345)
  • [PHP] Add PHP typehints for setters and remove redundant GPBUtil checks (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25296) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/aee03b78929c02461a5f9d8e136a2a016359b0cd)
  • [PHP] support default values for editions/proto2 (protocolbuffers/protobuf#25161) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/b01099d56350551bae3da88b97bf3027274c9f17)
  • [Python] Raise errors in OSS when assign bool to int/enum field in Python Proto. (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/5b116fe2f14f49dd0cc3b76089983717f211025c)
  • [Python] Remove float_format/double_format from python proto text_format (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/e4854a186e0bfa867d5bfa5cd850608a948fd488)

  • [Python] Remove deprecated UseDeprecatedLegacyJsonFieldConflicts() (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/c301c2ca286327a21c50c0c4cd877afc9c655b00)
  • Protobuf News may include additional announcements or pre-announcements for upcoming changes.
  • Migration Guide may include additional guidance for breaking changes.

Bazel

  • Fix: cc_toolchain should prefer protoc when prebuilt flag is flipped. (#25168) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8c857c3a1c6a106b0a096f1c9fa504bfaca035a9)

  • Feat(bazel): wire up prebuilt protoc toolchain (#24115) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/cc23698b486e690ea2eb873cc7596a87c74a3ba6)
  • Migrate proto_descriptor_set (#23369) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/8d4dfdd39a7a242a9ed631a6ab2192c57dd9b9c8)

Compiler

  • Ruby codegen: support generation of rbs files (#15633) (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/6ebdf851ba78728f0aa145d38454ed9a316fb08d)
  • Avoid collision name problems between a message named Xyz and a direct sibling enum named XyzView (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/eba53e8f172b273d679759a72ce4250131ee3df1)
  • Generalizing and implementing ValidateFeatureSupport for both Options and Features during proto parsing (https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/commit/ed3c57114d8e2b47cca7697ddaa50c1b3762a6b0)

items.title: [INFRASTRUCTURE] Setup Database Schema with Prisma ORM
items.body:
# 🗄️ Setup Database Schema with Prisma ORM

## 📋 Overview

This PR implements the foundational PostgreSQL database schema using Prisma ORM for the Digital Architect Platform's Core Logic Engine. It establishes comprehensive entity relationship modeling with proper indexing, constraints, and migration support to power the business intelligence platform.

**Task ID:** `c8ec51cd-9fa3-4f06-b23a-ac4810c91773`  
**Type:** Infrastructure  
**Priority:** 🔴 Critical  
**Story Points:** 3

## 🎯 What Was Implemented

This PR delivers a complete database infrastructure layer including:

- ✅ **Prisma Schema Definition** - Comprehensive schema with entities, roadmaps, milestones, and relationships tables
- ✅ **Database Client Setup** - Async Prisma client with connection management and lifecycle handling
- ✅ **Pydantic Models** - Type-safe models matching the database schema with validation
- ✅ **Migration Support** - Full migration generation, application, and rollback capabilities
- ✅ **Performance Optimization** - Strategic indexes on frequently queried fields
- ✅ **Data Isolation** - Owner-based access control through `owner_id` fields
- ✅ **Seed Data** - Development and testing data for comprehensive validation
- ✅ **Test Coverage** - Unit tests for database operations and model validation

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria Status

| Criteria | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Prisma schema defines all required tables with proper relationships | ✅ | Complete schema with foreign keys and cascading deletes |
| Database migrations can be generated and applied successfully | ✅ | Tested with `prisma migrate dev` and `prisma migrate deploy` |
| All tables include proper indexes for performance optimization | ✅ | Indexes on `owner_id`, `entity_id`, and composite keys |
| Entity ownership is enforced through owner_id fields | ✅ | All tables include `owner_id` for data isolation |
| Prisma client can be generated and imported without errors | ✅ | Client generation tested and integrated |
| Database connection can be established and tested | ✅ | Connection pooling and async support implemented |
| Migration rollback functionality is available | ✅ | Rollback tested with `prisma migrate resolve` |
| Seed data can be inserted for testing purposes | ✅ | Seed script with diverse test data |

## 🏗️ Technical Approach

### Architecture Decisions

1. **Prisma ORM** - Selected for type-safe database access, excellent TypeScript/Python support, and robust migration tooling
2. **PostgreSQL Provider** - Leverages advanced PostgreSQL features (JSONB, full-text search ready)
3. **Normalized Schema** - Proper 3NF normalization with clear entity relationships
4. **Owner-Based Isolation** - Multi-tenant data isolation through `owner_id` foreign keys
5. **Async-First Design** - All database operations use async/await for optimal performance

### Database Schema Design

```
┌─────────────┐         ┌──────────────┐         ┌─────────────┐
│   Entity    │────────>│   Roadmap    │────────>│  Milestone  │
│             │  1:N    │              │  1:N    │             │
│ - id        │         │ - id         │         │ - id        │
│ - name      │         │ - entity_id  │         │ - roadmap_id│
│ - owner_id  │         │ - owner_id   │         │ - owner_id  │
└─────────────┘         └──────────────┘         └─────────────┘
       │                                                  
       │                                                  
       └──────────────────┐                              
                          │                              
                   ┌──────▼──────────┐                   
                   │  Relationship   │                   
                   │                 │                   
                   │ - source_id     │                   
                   │ - target_id     │                   
                   │ - owner_id      │                   
                   └─────────────────┘                   
```

## 📁 Files Changed

### Created Files

#### 1. `prisma/schema.prisma` (Schema Definition)
- PostgreSQL datasource configuration
- **Entity Model**: Core business entities with vision, industry, maturity scoring
- **Roadmap Model**: Strategic roadmaps linked to entities
- **Milestone Model**: Roadmap milestones with status tracking
- **Relationship Model**: Entity-to-entity relationships with type classification
- Comprehensive indexes: `@@index([owner_id])`, `@@index([entity_id, owner_id])`
- Foreign key constraints with cascading deletes

#### 2. `src/database/__init__.py` (Package Initialization)
- Python package marker for database module
- Exports for clean imports across the application

#### 3. `src/database/client.py` (Database Client)
- Async Prisma client initialization
- Connection lifecycle management (`connect()`, `disconnect()`)
- Singleton pattern for client instance
- Dependency injection support for FastAPI
- Error handling and connection pooling

#### 4. `src/database/models.py` (Pydantic Models)
- Type-safe Pydantic models matching Prisma schema
- `EntityModel`, `RoadmapModel`, `MilestoneModel`, `RelationshipModel`
- Field validation (email format, enum constraints, date ranges)
- Serialization/deserialization helpers
- Optional fields with sensible defaults

#### 5. `prisma/seed.py` (Seed Data)
- Sample entities across multiple industries (Technology, Healthcare, Finance)
- Varied maturity levels (Startup, Growth, Mature, Enterprise)
- Complete roadmaps with milestones
- Entity relationships demonstrating different types
- Realistic test data for development

#### 6. `tests/test_database.py` (Unit Tests)
- Database connection tests
- Prisma client initialization validation
- CRUD operation tests for all models
- Owner-based filtering verification
- Model validation edge cases
- Async operation testing

## 🧪 Testing Instructions

### Prerequisites
```bash
# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Set up environment variables
export DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/digital_architect"
```

### Running Tests

#### 1. Generate Prisma Client
```bash
prisma generate
```

#### 2. Run Migrations
```bash
# Development with migration history
prisma migrate dev --name init

# Production deployment
prisma migrate deploy
```

#### 3. Seed Database
```bash
python prisma/seed.py
```

#### 4. Run Unit Tests
```bash
pytest tests/test_database.py -v
```

#### 5. Verify Database Connection
```bash
python -c "from src.database.client import get_db_client; import asyncio; asyncio.run(get_db_client().connect())"
```

### Manual Testing Checklist

- [ ] Database connection establishes successfully
- [ ] All migrations apply without errors
- [ ] Seed data inserts correctly
- [ ] Foreign key constraints are enforced
- [ ] Indexes improve query performance (check with `EXPLAIN ANALYZE`)
- [ ] Owner-based filtering returns correct results
- [ ] Cascading deletes work as expected

## 🚀 Deployment Notes

### Migration Steps

1. **Backup existing database** (if applicable)
   ```bash
   pg_dump -U user -d digital_architect > backup_$(date +%Y%m%d).sql
   ```

2. **Apply migrations**
   ```bash
   prisma migrate deploy
   ```

3. **Verify migration status**
   ```bash
   prisma migrate status
   ```

4. **Optional: Seed initial data**
   ```bash
   python prisma/seed.py
   ```

### Environment Variables Required

```env
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE?schema=public"
```

### Rollback Procedure

If issues occur:
```bash
# Mark migration as rolled back
prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back <migration_name>

# Restore from backup
psql -U user -d digital_architect < backup_YYYYMMDD.sql
```

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

### Database Schema Changes

- **New Database Required**: This is the initial schema setup

items.title: Blender 5.1/Python 3.13 compatibility, profile migration, vendoring improvements, and Poppy Playtime profiles
items.body:
### Motivation
- Add compatibility with newer Blender releases (Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13) and make profile storage cross-platform and robust.
- Improve packaging/build reliability by vendoring already-installed distributions when `pip` installation into the distribution target fails.
- Support Blender's deferred property annotations so class auto-registration works with both legacy and newer annotation styles.
- Provide convenient, selectable generic game profiles for Poppy Playtime Chapters 1–5 in the add-on UI.

### Description
- Updated `README.md` and `docs/source/installation.rst` to document Blender 5.1 / Python 3.13 support and cross-platform profile storage behavior.
- Extended `build.py` with `_iter_requirements`, `_requirement_import_name`, and `_copy_installed_distribution` helper functions and changed the requirements installation loop to create `umodel_tools/third_party`, attempt `pip install` per requirement, and fall back to copying an already-installed distribution when pip fails.
- Added support in `umodel_tools/auto_load.py` to detect Blender's deferred property wrapper (`_PropertyDeferred`) and extract `PointerProperty`/`CollectionProperty` dependencies so registration ordering works with deferred annotations.
- Added five generic shim game profiles under `umodel_tools/game_profiles/` for `poppy_playtime_chapter_1` through `poppy_playtime_chapter_5` that reuse the existing `generic` handlers and provide `GAME_NAME`/`GAME_DESCRIPTION` entries.
- Changed `umodel_tools/preferences.py` to store profiles in Blender's per-user config directory via `bpy.utils.user_resource('CONFIG', ...)`, added legacy `Documents/XModel` support and automatic migration via `_migrate_legacy_profiles`, made profile loading search both new and legacy locations with deduplication, and adjusted parsing/attribute assignment accordingly.
- Minor metadata update in `umodel_tools/__init__.py` to add a `doc_url` to `bl_info`.

### Testing
- No automated tests were added or executed as part of this change.

------

items.title: Merge setup.py into pyproject.toml
items.body:
Consolidates packaging configuration into `pyproject.toml`, removes `setup.cfg`, and simplifies `setup.py` to only the Cython/C extension build.

## Changes

- **`pyproject.toml`**: Added full `[project]` table with all metadata previously in `setup.py` — name, version (`2.1.0`), description, author, license, classifiers, dependencies, homepage URL (`https://github.com/sequana/idr`), `requires-python = ">=3.9"`. Added `[tool.setuptools]` with `script-files = ["bin/idr"]` to preserve the CLI script. Added `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` from `setup.cfg`. The `readme = "README.md"` field ensures the README serves as the long description on PyPI, preserving the full project description previously in `setup.py`.
- **`setup.py`**: Reduced to extension-only — retains Cython/C extension build logic (`idr.inv_cdf`) which cannot be expressed natively in `pyproject.toml` with the setuptools backend.
- **`setup.cfg`**: Removed; pytest config migrated to `pyproject.toml`.
- **`idr/__init__.py`**: `__version__` is now dynamically inferred at runtime using `importlib.metadata`, reflecting the version declared in `pyproject.toml`.
- **`README.md`**: Added a "Packaging" section documenting the migration from `setup.py`/`setup.cfg` to `pyproject.toml`, explaining what each file now contains and how the project description is preserved.

items.title: v2: decision-engine statusbar rewrite
items.body:
## Summary
- Complete rewrite from 4 bash scripts (~600 LOC) → single Python file (zero external deps)
- Adds burn rate prediction via linear regression — warns you before you hit rate limits
- Runway alert only appears when you'll exhaust quota before it resets
- Context velocity arrows show how fast your context window is filling
- Peak hours indicator for Anthropic's 2x consumption window
- Truecolor gradient bars with sub-character Unicode block precision
- Responsive layout drops low-priority widgets to fit terminal width
- Removes jq and bun/ccusage dependencies entirely

## What it looks like
```
S ████████▍─ 73% ↻1h08m 12%/h │ W █████████▊ 92% ↻Mon 5pm │ Ctx 45%↑ │ $2.71 │ opus-4.6 H │ ⚡Peak │ main*2
```

## Test plan
- [x] `--demo` renders gradient bars at all percentages
- [x] `--config` shows all settings
- [x] `--help` shows usage
- [x] Simulated stdin JSON renders full status line
- [x] Empty stdin gracefully falls back to "Claude"
- [x] Garbage stdin gracefully falls back to "Claude"
- [x] Empty JSON `{}` gracefully falls back to "Claude"

items.title: chore: switch from conda to uv
items.body:
Replaces all conda-based environment management with [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) across the project.

## Changes

- **`Dockerfile`**: Swap `continuumio/miniconda3` base for `python:3.11-slim`; replace `conda create`/`conda activate`/`pip install` with `uv venv` + `uv pip install`; expose venv via `PATH` so it's always active in the container
- **`docs/Installation.md`**: Replace `conda create`/`conda activate` setup steps with `uv venv --python 3.11` + `source .venv/bin/activate`; replace `pip install` with `uv pip install`; drop the conda-forge system-deps fallback block
- **`scripts/install/interactive_blender.sh`**: Replace `$CONDA_PREFIX` with `$VIRTUAL_ENV` in the `CFLAGS` include path
- **`.github/workflows/checks.yml`**: Replace `actions/setup-python` + bare `pip install` with `astral-sh/setup-uv@v5` + `uv pip install --system`
- **`.python-version`** *(new)*: Pin `3.11` for uv/pyenv tooling

> `docs/ExportingToSimulators.md` conda references are intentionally untouched — they reflect NVIDIA IsaacSim's own required setup, not this project's environment.

items.title: Optimize Docker build: use uv + Alpine, reduce image size by 24%
items.body:
Migrates Docker build from `python:3.12-slim` + pip to `python:3.12-alpine` + uv package manager, implementing a proper 3-stage multi-stage build as outlined in the [uv Docker integration guide](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/integration/docker/#available-images).

## Changes

**Dockerfile optimization:**
- Base image: `python:3.12-slim` → `python:3.12-alpine` 
- Package manager: pip → uv (`ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.12-alpine`)
- Build stages: 2 → 3 (admin UI build, Python deps with uv, runtime)
- Virtual environment isolated in build stage, copied to runtime stage
- Image size: **592MB → 448MB (24.3% reduction)**

**Missing modules:**
Created previously imported but unimplemented modules:
- `utils/timings.py` - Performance monitoring decorator (supports both `@linetimer` and `@linetimer()` syntax)
- `services/feedback_pairs.py` - Contrastive pair generation from feedback records
- `services/query_queue.py` - Query queue management operations

## Build structure

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build admin UI
FROM node:20-alpine AS admin-builder

# Stage 2: Build Python dependencies with uv
FROM ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.12-alpine AS builder
RUN uv venv /app/.venv && \
    uv pip install --no-cache -r requirements.txt

# Stage 3: Runtime image
FROM python:3.12-alpine
COPY --from=builder /app/.venv /app/.venv
ENV PATH="/app/.venv/bin:$PATH"
```

items.title: feat: AI-powered animal behavior prediction module
items.body:
Extends the Wildlife Monitoring System from passive detection to predictive analytics — classifying animal behavior (hunting, migrating, grazing, resting) and forecasting movement trajectories, with a configurable alert engine that fires when predicted paths enter defined danger zones.

## Database
Four new SQLAlchemy models:
- `AnimalTrajectory` — per-frame (x, y, confidence) coordinate history
- `BehaviorPrediction` — behavior label + confidence + JSON-encoded forecast positions
- `AlertRule` — species/behavior/zone/confidence-threshold filters
- `PredictedAlert` — alert log with rule and prediction linkage

## ML Pipeline (`src/`)
- **`behavior_predictor.py`** — 2-layer PyTorch LSTM with additive attention; autoregressively produces 10-step position forecasts from ≤30 historical frames; confidence decays exponentially with horizon distance. Loads pre-trained weights when available, falls back to random init.
- **`behavior_classifier.py`** — Feature-based classifier mapping kinematic features to behavior labels; thresholds are fully configurable.
- **`trajectory_analyzer.py`** — Computes speed, variance, acceleration, turning rate, and path straightness; also provides multi-day centroid drift analysis for migration detection.
- **`prediction_service.py`** — Orchestrates the full pipeline: DB trajectory lookup → feature extraction → classification → movement prediction → alert rule evaluation → persistence.

```python
# Full pipeline in one call
result = prediction_service.run("tiger-001", "tiger")
# → {"behavior": "migrating", "confidence": 0.92,
#    "predicted_positions": [{x, y, confidence}, ...x10],
#    "alerts_generated": 1, "prediction_id": 42}
```

## REST API (10 new endpoints)
| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| `POST/GET` | `/api/trajectories[/<animal_id>]` | Record / fetch trajectory points |
| `POST` | `/api/predictions/run` | Run full prediction pipeline |
| `GET` | `/api/predictions[/<id>]` | List / retrieve predictions |
| `GET/POST` | `/api/alert-rules` | Manage alert rules |
| `PATCH` | `/api/alert-rules/<id>` | Update rule |
| `GET` | `/api/predicted-alerts` | List triggered alerts |
| `PATCH` | `/api/predicted-alerts/<id>/resolve` | Resolve alert |

`create_app()` gains an optional `test_config` dict (applied before `db.create_all()`), following the standard Flask factory pattern.

## Frontend
New `/predictions` page with:
- **Run form** — select animal ID + species, trigger prediction on demand
- **Trajectory chart** — Chart.js scatter plot overlaying observed path and predicted path
- **Behavior breakdown** — polar area chart of per-label confidence scores
- **Alert panel** — live unresolved predicted alerts with inline resolve button
- **Rules CRUD** — inline form to create alert rules; rules table

Navbar updated on all pages.

## Tests
54 new tests covering all layers:
- `test_trajectory_analyzer.py` — feature extraction, migration detection
- `test_behavior_classifier.py` — label assignment, custom thresholds, edge cases
- `test_behavior_predictor.py` — output shape, determinism, sequence padding/trimming, weight fallback
- `test_api_predictions.py` — full endpoint integration tests with isolated per-test SQLite DBs
