<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Overview
Enhance the Wildlife Monitoring System with advanced AI-powered animal behavior prediction capabilities. This feature will transition from simple detection to intelligent predictive analytics.

## Features to Implement

### 1. Behavior Prediction Module
- **Movement Direction Prediction**: LSTM/Transformer-based temporal model to predict where animals are heading
- **Hunting Behavior Detection**: Classify animal behavior (hunting, grazing, migrating, resting)
- **Migration Pattern Analysis**: Identify seasonal patterns and long-term movement trends
- **Alert System**: Trigger notifications when animals move toward populated areas

### 2. Core Components

#### A. Database Schema Updates (PostgreSQL)
- `animal_trajectories` table: Store coordinate history with timestamps
- `behavior_predictions` table: Store predicted behaviors with confidence scores
- `alert_rules` table: Configurable rules for different species and behaviors
- `predicted_alerts` table: Log generated alerts

#### B. Python Backend (Flask)
- `models/behavior_predictor.py`: LSTM model for movement prediction
- `models/behavior_classifier.py`: CNN/ML classifier for hunting/behavior detection
- `services/prediction_service.py`: Orchestrates predictions and alerts
- `routes/predictions.py`: REST API endpoints
- `utils/trajectory_analyzer.py`: Analyzes movement patterns

#### C. Frontend (React/JavaScript)
- Trajectory visualization with predicted paths
- Behavior classification display with confidence metrics
- Alert notification system with danger zones
- Historical pattern charts

## Technical Details

### Movement Prediction Model
- Input: Last 30 frames of animal coordinates (x, y, confidence)
- Output: Next 10 frames predicted coordinates + confidence
- Architecture: LSTM with 2 hidden layers, attention mechanism
- Training data: Historical tracking data from detection system

### Behavior Classification
- Input: Movement features (speed, acceleration, direction changes, spatial context)
- Output: Behavior class (hunting, migrating, grazing, resting, other)
- Uses ensemble of features: trajectory curvature, speed variance, proximity to resources

### Alert Logic
- Configurable danger zones (villages, roads, human settlements)
- Species-specific threat levels
- Alert escalation based on prediction confidence

## Implementation Steps

1. Create database migrations for new tables
2. Implement behavior prediction models (Python/TensorFlow)
3. Build prediction service with real-time processing
4. Add REST API endpoints for predictions and alerts
5. Create React components for visualization
6. Add comprehensive tests and documentation
7. Deploy with Docker configuration

## Success Criteria
- Behavior predictions return results in <2 seconds (maintain existing performance)
- Achieve 85%+ accuracy on held-out test dataset
- Alert system detects dangerous proximity with 90%+ precision
- System handles 1000+ animals simultaneously
- Full integration with existing detection pipeline

Release 0.6.3

It's a minor release.

  • Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (CVE-2026-30922).
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field.
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes.
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.2

It's a minor release.

  • Fixed continuation octet limits in OID/RELATIVE-OID decoder (CVE-2026-23490).
  • Added support for Python 3.14.
  • Added SECURITY.md policy.
  • Migrated to pyproject.toml packaging.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for Python 3.13.
  • Cleaned Python 2-related code.
  • Removed bdist_wheel universal flag from setup.cfg.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.6.0

It's a major release where we drop Python 2 support entirely. The most significant changes are:

  • Removed support for EOL Python 2.7, 3.6, 3.7
  • Added support for previously missing RELATIVE-OID construct
  • Updated link to Layman's Guide

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Release 0.5.1

It's a minor release.

  • Added support for PyPy 3.10 and Python 3.12
  • Updated RTD configuration to include a dummy index.rst redirecting to contents.html, ensuring compatibility with third-party documentation and search indexes.
  • Fixed the API breakage wih decoder.decode(substrateFun=...). A substrateFun passed to decoder.decode() can now be either v0.4 Non-Streaming or v0.5 Streaming. pyasn1 will detect and handle both cases transparently. A substrateFun passed to one of the new streaming decoders is still expected to be v0.5 Streaming only.

items.title: Project scaffolding – Flask app factory, config, and database setup
items.body:
Bootstraps the entire Python project from an empty repo: Poetry project, Flask app factory, SQLite database layer, schema, smoke tests, and CI.

### Project config
- `pyproject.toml` — Poetry with `Flask ^3.1`, `APScheduler ^3.10`, `requests ^2.31`, `openai ^2.0`, `python-dotenv ^1.0`; dev deps `pytest ^8.0`, `mypy ^1.0`
- `config.py` — all settings read from env vars (`OPENAI_API_BASE`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL` → `data/metadata.db`, `SECRET_KEY`, cron intervals)
- `.env.example` — placeholder values for all required variables with inline time-unit comments; cron defaults: metadata daily (1440 min), ontologies monthly (720 h), tools weekly (168 h)

### Application factory (`app/__init__.py`)
- `create_app(config=None)` loads `config.py`, wires DB teardown, registers CLI, starts `BackgroundScheduler` (suppressed when `TESTING=True`)

### Database layer
- `app/db/sqlite.py` — uses `pathlib.Path` throughout (no `os`); `get_db()` (Flask `g`-scoped connection, works for both HTTP requests and APScheduler background jobs via `with app.app_context()`); `init_db()` (idempotent, executes `schema.sql`, not a migration tool); `close_db()` (teardown hook); no sqlite-vector extension (deferred to ontology issue)
- `app/db/schema.sql` — `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` for `users`, `sessions`, `metadata_cache`, `semantic_tools`
  - `metadata_cache` stores only URL-level crawl state (`content_hash`, `structural_summary`, `last_crawled_at`) — no `result_json`, since results are per-user (influenced by individual prompts) and live exclusively in `sessions.result_json`
- `flask db init` CLI command calls `init_db()`

### Tests (`tests/test_app.py`)
Five smoke tests covering app instantiation, config override, table creation, and idempotent `init_db()` calls — all using an in-memory SQLite database.

```python
app = create_app({"TESTING": True, "DATABASE_URL": ":memory:"})
# init_db() creates users / sessions / metadata_cache / semantic_tools
# calling init_db() twice does not raise (IF NOT EXISTS guards)
```

### CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)
GitHub Actions workflow running `pytest tests/ -v` and `mypy app tests` on every push and pull request.

items.title: Add Wikipedia command, structured logging, proper error handling, and message splitting to GopalBot
items.body:
GopalBot used a bare `discord.Client` with `print()` logging, silent 2000-char response truncation, no prefix commands, and no Wikipedia support. This rewrites `bot.py` to a production-ready structure.

## Core changes

- **`discord.ext.commands.Bot`** — migrated from raw client; all features now use `@bot.command` decorators with docstrings surfaced via `!help`
- **`!wiki <query>`** — Wikipedia integration returning a 3-sentence summary as a rich embed (title, URL, footer); handles `DisambiguationError` and `PageError` with user-friendly messages
- **`!ask <question>`** — explicit prefix command for Grok AI queries, decoupled from mention/name/DM triggers (which are preserved)
- **Message splitting** — replaces `reply[:1997] + "..."` with `split_message()` that breaks on newlines and sends multiple messages
- **Structured logging** — `logging.basicConfig` with timestamp + level + logger name, replacing all `print()` calls
- **Error handling** — `on_command_error` for `MissingRequiredArgument` (names the missing param), `CommandNotFound` (echoes the attempted command), and a generic fallback; env var validation raises `EnvironmentError` at startup if either token is absent
- **Bot presence** — sets "Listening to !help" on `on_ready`
- **Case-insensitive name stripping** — uses `re.sub(..., flags=re.IGNORECASE)` instead of two sequential `.replace()` calls

## Files changed

- `bot.py` — full rewrite
- `requirements.txt` — adds `wikipedia==1.4.0`
- `README.md` — updated feature list, commands table, dependencies
- `.gitignore` — added (excludes `__pycache__`, `*.pyc`, `.env`)

```python
# Before: silent truncation
if len(reply) > 2000:
    reply = reply[:1997] + "..."

# After: split at newlines, send multiple messages
def split_message(text: str, limit: int = DISCORD_MAX_LENGTH) -> list[str]:
    chunks = []
    while len(text) > limit:
        split_at = text.rfind("\n", 0, limit)
        if split_at == -1:
            split_at = limit
        chunks.append(text[:split_at])
        text = text[split_at:].lstrip("\n")
    if text:
        chunks.append(text)
    return chunks
```

items.title: feat: Transform into full-stack interactive dashboard with React Force-Graph and FastAPI
items.body:
## Overview
This PR transforms the `agentic-knowledge-web` project from basic CLI scripts into a complete, interactive full-stack web application. It introduces a FastAPI backend layer to expose agent capabilities as REST endpoints and a modern React (Vite) frontend featuring a dynamic force-directed knowledge graph visualization.

## Key Changes

### 1. Backend (FastAPI)
- **API Layer**: Implemented full ASGI FastAPI application in `main.py` overriding the empty placeholder.
- **Routers**: Added modular REST endpoints under `src/api/` for `/chat`, `/ingest`, `/graph`, and `/reset`.
- **Dependency Injection**: Added `deps.py` for efficient Neo4j, Chroma, and LLM singleton management.
- **Graph Queries**: Enhanced `Neo4jClient` with frontend-ready graph serialization (`get_graph_data` & `expand_node`).
- **Resilience**: Added error handling around vector retrieval to gracefully handle Ollama `NaN` generation timeouts, allowing fallback to pure Graph search.
- **Model Config**: Switched local embedding model to `nomic-embed-text`.

### 2. Frontend (React + Vite)
- **Initialization**: Bootstrapped a new React + Vite project under `frontend/`.
- **Styling**: Configured deep dark themes, glassmorphism UI, and gradient components using Tailwind CSS v3.
- **State Management**: Implemented `Zustand` (`useStore.js`) for managing deeply nested graph and chat states globally.
- **Interactive Knowledge Web**: Integrated `react-force-graph-2d` to visualize RAG components.
  - **Dynamic Expansion**: Users can now right-click nodes to pull and merge localized neighbors incrementally.
  - **Auto Interaction**: Clicking nodes displays context data along with quick "Ask AI" integrations.
- **Integrations**: Fully wired up Markdown-supported chat window and drag-and-drop ingestion endpoints.

### 3. Build & Docs
- **Dependencies**: Updated `pyproject.toml` and lock files incorporating `fastapi` and `uvicorn`. 
- **Doc Rewrite**: Refactored `README.md` to reflect the new dual-server (FastAPI + Vite) startup flow and latest architectural diagram.

## How to Test
1. Start backend: `uv run python -m uvicorn main:app --reload`
2. Start frontend: `cd frontend && npm run dev`
3. Hit `http://localhost:5173`.
4. Drop a markdown file onto the bottom-left uploader to trigger knowledge ingestion.
5. Watch the Graph automatically reload, zoom across entities, right-click to expand edges, and interact with the Agent via chat.

## Notes
- **Breaking Change**: The embedding model was swapped to `nomic-embed-text` (dim: 768). If migrating an existing local environment, please press the danger "Reset" button on the UI (or manually delete `chroma_db/`) to wipe old 1024-dim `bge-m3` vectors.

items.title: Replace custom ModelGenerator with sqlacodegen subclass
items.body:
## Summary

- Replace the 637-line custom `ModelGenerator` with `SmtGenerator`, a subclass of sqlacodegen's `DeclarativeGenerator` (~350 lines, ~12 method overrides)
- `ModelGenerator` in `smt/models.py` becomes a thin ~120-line wrapper (reflection, backup, file I/O)
- sqlacodegen added as pip dependency (`>=4.0,<5.0`) — not a fork, upstream fixes flow in automatically
- All 11 changes from `docs/SQLACODEGEN_FORK.md` spec implemented
- All docs updated to reflect new architecture

### SmtGenerator method overrides

| Change | Methods |
|---|---|
| Multi-file output | `generate()` returns `dict[str, str]` |
| Lowercase identifiers | `render_class_variables()`, `render_column_attribute()`, `render_constraint()` |
| PascalCase Python attrs | `generate_column_attr_name()` |
| Target schema rewriting | `render_table_args()`, `render_constraint()` |
| Collation stripping | `fix_column_types()` |
| Type fallback (MSSQL) | `get_adapted_type()` + `_MSSQL_TYPE_OVERRIDES` |
| Identity() for PK | `render_column_attribute()` |
| Keyword escaping | `generate_column_attr_name()`, `generate_model_name()` |
| No relationships | `generate_relationships()` returns `[]` |
| File headers | `_generate_file_header()` |
| Backup | Stays in `ModelGenerator` wrapper |

## Test plan

- [x] 86 unit tests pass (up from 56) — 21 new SmtGenerator tests, 9 updated wrapper tests
- [x] Lint clean (`ruff check src/ tests/`)
- [x] PG-to-PG e2e: 3 tables with FKs migrated, all identifiers lowercase, Identity on PKs
- [x] MSSQL-to-PG e2e: NVARCHAR→String, NTEXT→Text, MONEY→Numeric, BIT→Boolean, DATETIME2→DateTime, collation stripped
- [x] Pipeline backward-compatible — `ModelGenerator.__init__` + `write()` interface unchanged

- Migrate setup.py/setup.cfg → pyproject.toml (Python ≥ 3.9, semver 0.9.0)
- Refactor rtsp_curl.py: • Rename Rtsp_Curl → RtspCurl and file_sdp → internal helper (PEP 8) • Add type hints and docstrings throughout • Replace DEBUG global with stdlib logging • Move port-pair allocation to init() (no module-level side effects) • Fix get_sdp_filename bug: file was closed after first curl chunk; now opened/closed around the full perform() call • Fix get_media_control_attribute: correctly returns media-level control attribute (skips session-level wildcard) with clear error messages • Add _require_init() guard to all methods

items.title: feat(launch): migrate ROS 1 XML launch files to ROS 2 Python scripts
items.body:
## Description
This PR addresses the final Stage (Developer 3) of our ROS 1 to ROS 2 Migration Plan. It converts all legacy [.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0) XML files into ROS 2 compliant [.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0) scripts.

## Changes Made
*   **Deleted Legacy Files:** Safely removed outdated ROS 1 `*.launch` files from the [launch/](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0) directory ([1_world_and_script.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world_and_script.launch:0:0-0:0), [2_world_and_script.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world_and_script.launch:0:0-0:0), [1_world.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch:0:0-0:0), [2_world.launch](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch:0:0-0:0)).
*   **Created Python Replacements:**
    *   **Gazebo Simulation ([1_world.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0), [2_world.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world.launch.py:0:0-0:0))**: Included the `gazebo.launch.py` file from `gazebo_ros` and pointed it to the correct custom `.world` file within our package via the `world` launch argument.
    *   **Full Suite ([1_world_and_script.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/1_world_and_script.launch.py:0:0-0:0), [2_world_and_script.launch.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/launch/2_world_and_script.launch.py:0:0-0:0))**: Defined an `IncludeLaunchDescription` for Gazebo and instantiated a ROS 2 `Node` for [camera_read.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/scripts/camera_read.py:0:0-0:0) (and [decode_qr.py](cci:7://file:///home/pouya/pkg_cv_ros_tutorial_by_dhanuzch/scripts/decode_qr.py:0:0-0:0) respectively) to run in parallel. 
    *   **Environment Variables:** Automatically mapped the `models` directory to the `GAZEBO_MODEL_PATH` explicitly via the `SetEnvironmentVariable` action, ensuring Gazebo correctly discovers locally defined models (like the QR code plate) across machines.

## Dependencies
*   Depends on Stage 1 (ament_cmake refactoring) & Stage 2 (rclpy nodes integration).

items.title: feat: incorporate MAS best-practices — AGENTS.md, contracts, evals, dev tooling
items.body:
Enterprise agentic SaaS repos commonly lack inspectable behavior surfaces, centralized interface contracts, and contributor guides tuned for multi-agent systems. This PR closes those gaps against the P0/P1/P2 prioritized checklist.

## P0 — Behavior inspectable, contracts centralized

- **`AGENTS.md`** — MAS-specific contributor guide: repo map, change safety rules (what breaks production and why), how to add skills/agents/providers, required `make verify` gate, debugging with OTel traces
- **`contracts/tool-manifests/`** — versioned JSON Schema for `semantic_search`, `evaluate_formula`, `graph_traverse`, `generate_business_case`; these are now the authoritative source of truth for tool input/output shapes
- **`contracts/jsonschema/entity.json`** — shared ontology entity schema
- **`tests/contract/test_tool_manifests.py`** — parametrized tests asserting every manifest is structurally valid and has a corresponding `skills/` or `workflows/` definition (prevents schema drift)
- **Root `README.md`** — monorepo entry point with ≤7-command quickstart and full repo map (previously absent; `value-fabric/README.md` was the only entry point)

## P1 — Contributor infrastructure

- **`CONTRIBUTING.md`** — dev setup, per-layer test categories (unit/integration/e2e/contract/evals), commit format (Conventional Commits)
- **`SECURITY.md`** — supported versions, private disclosure process, response SLAs, security design principles (HMAC vs bcrypt rationale, audit log append-only enforcement, OIDC in CI)
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — SemVer-based, Unreleased section seeded with this PR's additions, prior release history backfilled
- **`tests/evals/`** — golden-trace evaluation scaffold: `conftest.py`, JSON fixtures for `semantic_search` and `evaluate_formula`, skill-level contract tests validating fixture invariants (scenario ordering, type constraints); 25 tests, all pass

## P2 — Dev ergonomics and supply-chain security

- **`Makefile`** — `make verify` (lint + typecheck + tests), `make evals`, `make migrate`, `make contracts`, `make up/down/logs/clean`; one command to confirm the repo is healthy
- **`.github/dependabot.yml`** — automated dependency PRs for all 6 pip layers, npm frontend, and GitHub Actions on weekly cadence

## What was not changed

Runtime/provider separation, per-layer CI with 80% coverage gates, shared identity library, K8s manifests, monitoring stack, `.env.example`, and agent behavior-as-Markdown artifacts were already sound. No existing tests were modified.
